titlestmt.titleproper: Michael L. and Catherine H. Whalen Collection of Cornelliana37-5-3784
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Michael Whalen

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-12-15 18:16:52 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Michael L. and Catherine H. Whalen Collection of Cornelliana
did.physdesc: Computer Media, Memorabilia, Manuscripts, Photographs, Scrapbooks
did.abstract: Photographs, including carte-de-visite pictures, cabinet cards, stereo views, prints and negatives. Also scrapbooks.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Ephemera, manuscripts and other Cornelliana. Photographs, including carte-de-visite pictures, cabinet cards, stereo views, prints and negatives. Subjects include individual students and faculty, notably William Benjamin Bowler, a student from Haiti, ca. 1870 and architecture students, ca. 1914; campus buildings and scenes; David Woodbury King and the Cornell Crew,1876; and waterfalls and other local scenes. Also a DVD containing scans of many of the photographs as well as a catalog of the items.

Letters, including a set of from Roger Burchard Platt to his mother, 1907; materials relating to the Winter Short Course taken by farmer George S. Allnatt in 1905; letter from the Cornell Treasurer's Office to J. W. Williams about land grant transactions, 1875; TLS from Charles Kendall Adams, 1889.

Broadsides, handbills, programs (Cornell Masque, 1922-1923 and Cornell Dramatic Club, 1925); invitations, and menus relating to student activities, ca. 1889-1925; diploma for Leroy Lynn Davis (Civil Engineering), 1896; "The Cornell Earache," Spring Day parody issue, 1908; Registers and other university publications;Freshman Register, 1975; Interfraternity Council Rush Book, 1973; "Book of Views," 1925; various issues of the Cornell Daily Sun and the Cornell Graphic.
Lecture notebooks in Chemistry (Cicero Shaffer, 1875); and American History with Moses Coit Tyler (Sarah McCune Gallaher, 1893); and Mechanical Engineering drawings by Frank Cook Moore, 1890; "

Scrapbooks of Henry Vernon Davis, 1911; Lily Hawley, 1915; John Neal Tilton, 1915; Marion Ruthella Roberts, 1920; Helen Levengood, Summer School, 1921; Leon Wendell Walton, 1922; and Marion Helen Rogers, 1923.
Correspondence written and received by Dorothy McCormack (Class of 1937) while she was a student at Cornell, mostly to her future husband Allan B. Grady, Jr.
Letter, January 7, 1882, from John Newton Dexter Shinkel (Class of 1881) to Josiah Dustin Wilson (Class of 1880), concerning a recent visit to Ithaca, NY and news from Cornell.

Diaries kept by Hart Irving Seely (Class of 1909), who studied both law and agriculture at Cornell, 1906-1907, 1912-1913; notebook kept by George Meade Emory, (LLB 1890); scrapbook of Nicholas Cooke Cushing (Class of 1896), 1893.

Individual photographs include: photograph of Benjamin Harry Weisbrod (Class of 1914); William Hazlitt Smith (Class of 1873); William Maddocks (Class of 1887); Robert Elmer Rutherford (Class of 1887), Thomas Hall (ME 1893, MME 1894), and other 19th century cabinet card photographs.
Group of cabinet card photographs, primarily of members of the Class of 1889, originally collected by Albert Haywood '89.
Tintypes of early students including Simon Henry Gage.
Stock certificate for the New York State Agriculture College in Ovid, N.Y..
Cornell Music Festival programs.
Collection of wax seals of campus organizations.

Notebooks of law school student Robert Grantier Miller Class of 1940. Freshman registers. Notebook used by Jefferson Mack Myers, Class of 1876, for his human physiology and hygiene class. The Official Guide to the Schoellkopf Sweepstakes, Spring Day 1923. Clarence Isaac Robinson Class of 1884 materials. Photos from the Vermont Academy. Tintype of Taughannock Falls.

Poster for the 3rd Annual Ovation of the Citizens of Ithaca, to Cornell's Victors, 1877. Harold Carl Harding, Class of 1910 notebook for Course XII in mechanical engineering in the Sibley College, Chemistry Department faculty photo, 1921, portrait of the Class of 1895, letters to servicemen in World War Two from Marie Ann Tipula, Class of 1947, Peck Shoe Store photo, photos relating to George W. Brownell and the Ithaca trolley car company.
Many additional photos, scrapbooks, letters, notebooks, and ephemera.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIAL
relatedmaterial.p: See also New York State College of Agriculture Winter Short Course Scrapbooks, #21-34-4029.
See also the New York State College Of Agriculture Winter Short Course Photographs, #21-34-2459.
See also Dairy Industry Winter Course Student Record Cards, #21-19-1190.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Note that items next to each other in boxes do not necessarily come from the same person or have the same provenance. See the PDF guide to determine groupings.

odd.head: General
odd.p: See the catalog linked below for a complete inventory of the collection, the box listing below does not cover all the items.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Michael L. and Catherine H. Whalen Collection of Cornelliana, #37-5-3784. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Greek letter societies -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Students, Foreign -- Haiti.
Women college students -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.

unittitle.extref: An extensive photo and name index to this collection, with additional information on the items, is available here. 50 MB PDF
High resolution version of the catalog is available here. 220 MB PDF

did.unittitle: Index to the collection and digital images

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes high resolution copy of the PDF guide available above. Also JPG images of items in the collection. High resolution TIF files are available from the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carte de visites and cabinet cards including images of alumni and faculty, William Benjamin Bowler, crew, stereo view cards of campus, etc..

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cabinet cards and photographs of campus and Ithaca area, including Taughannock Falls, gorges, Cayuga Lake, alumni, Spring Day, White Hall architecture studio, etc..

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs including Alpha Theta fraternity, letters from students, administrators, and others, Freshman Banquet and other handbills, ad for the Kornell Krinkles, Globe Hotel Syracuse menu, event programs including Ulysses of Ithaka by the Cornell Masque, The Contrast by Cornell Dramatic Club, etc..

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Felt banner and reunion buttons of Mary Lucetta Thompson Davis Class of 1896.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1896 diploma of Lynn Leroy Davis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cornell registers, reports, The Cornell Earache, 1904 directory, chemistry class notebook of Cicero Shaffer Class of 1878, American history class notebook of Sarah McCune Gallaher, Class of 1895, 1975 freshman register, 1972 Interfraternity Council Rush Book, etc..

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three bound books of mechanical drawings created by Frank Cook Moore, Class of 1892, for engineering classes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fund-raising "position statement" issued by the Cornellian Council in 1925 entitled, "The Solution"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book of views, 1890s Cornell Daily Suns, 1906 Cornell Deadly Sin, 1866 Harper's Weekly about Ezra Cornell, six issues of the 1924-1925 Cornell Graphic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Lily Hawley, Class of 1919

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by John Neal Tilton Jr., Class of 1913

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Leon Wendell Walton, Class of 1926

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Marion Helen Rogers, Class of 1927

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1927 group portrait of the Sigma Kappa Sorority

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Marion Ruthella Roberts, Class of 1924

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1922 and 1923 group portrait of the Kappa Delta Sorority

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Henry Vernon Davis, Class of 1914 and other material owned by him

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Henry Marx, Class of 1879

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Helen Levengood during her attendance at a Summer School program in music at Cornell University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photo composite of students in the Winter Course of 1918-19 in the College of Agriculture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence written and received by Dorothy McCormack, Class of 1937, while she was a student

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from John Newton Dexter Shinkel, Class of 1881, to Josiah Dustin Wilson, Class of 1882, four diaries kept by law and agriculture student Hart Irving Seely, Class of 1909, Notebook used by George Meade Emory, Class of 1890, during his classes in the Cornell Law School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook compiled by Nicholas Cooke Cushing, Class of 1896,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of students and others, many owned by Albert Haywood, Class of 1889,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Alexander Glencairn Acheson, Class of 1917

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Le Roy William Pritchard, Class of 1922

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence of Marion Ruthella Roberts, Class of 1924

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laboratory notebooks and class project reports for a variety of courses created by Frederick Edgar Stark, Class of 1912

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of the Class of 1887

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various ephemera, stereo view photographs of campus, other campus and Ithaca photographs including military, mud rush, student and faculty carte de visites and cabinet cards, photo of Ezra Cornell Class of 1971,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1897 photo of Kappa Alpha Fraternity

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Carl Frederick Meyer, Class of 1908, ten-inch Keuffel and Esser mahogany slide rule and eight-inch leather case owned by Gustav Adolphus Marius Illmer, Class of 1909

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various ephemera including shingles, letters, programs, state examination booklets, 1892 University Corps of Cadets roster, Telegram sent by George H. Conklin to William Henry Peer Conklin, Class of 1895, concerning George's military service during the Civil War, 1960 Freshman Register, 1960 description of Cornell's fraternities, postcards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs including carte de visites and stereo view cards of students, faculty (including Ezra Cornell), staff, campus buildings and the Ithaca area, 1875 course book, letters, program for the Thirtieth Annual Contest for the '86 Memorial Prize in Declamation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by Edward John Burns Jr., Class of 1917, scrapbook assembled by Henry Godley, Class of 1924, Alpha Tau Omega fraternity photos, photo of Thomas Frederick Crane and others playing tennis on the arts quad

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1941 diary belonging to Theodore Ryder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1913-1914 scrapbook assembled by Frank (?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program for Music Festival 1916 Cornell University April 27 28 29

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various ephemera including letters, certificates, telegrams, tintype of Simon Henry Gage, stereo view cards of campus, cabinet cards of students, other photos and tintype of students and campus and Ezra Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Programs for Music Festival 1920 Cornell University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of 16 brass wax seal stamps of student organization logos used for shingles and certificates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Certificate of membership in the Cornell Chapter of Acacia Fraternity issued to David William Cheney, Class of 1922, photograph of the special Lehigh Valley Railroad, Auburn & Ithaca Branch crew spectator train, ca. 1920 photograph of the brothers of the Alpha Tau Omega Fraternity

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diplomas and certificates relating to Vining Campbell Dunlap Class of 1916 and others,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook assembled by James Aloysius Barrett, Class of 1912

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of letters and other documents belonging to Mary Ellen Gillett, Class of 1941

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Certificate appointing of Edward Benedict Clark, Class of 1912, as a principal musician in the Cornell University Corps of Cadets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Felt arm patch and brass pin belonging to Edward Benedict Clark, Class of 1912, as a principal musician in the Cornell University Corps of Cadets and photograph of him with clarinet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various pamphlets, photographs, letters, and other ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stereo-view, cabinet cards, carte de visite photographs, tintyps of the Cornell campus, Ithaca, McGraw-Fiske mansion, Ezra's tunnel, waterfalls, Cornell students and faculty, many photos owned by James Duncan Upham, some related to Zeta Psi fraternity

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Set of 11 notebooks used by Robert Grantier Miller, Law School Class of 1940, for legal classes at Cornell University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of the twentieth anniversary reunion of the Class of 1894

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1956, 1960-1964, 1975 Freshman registers, pig books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Menu card for the Cornell University Medical College Class of 1904 senior banquet that was held at the Café Boulevard in New York City on 12 Feb 1904

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook used by Jefferson Mack Myers, Class of 1876, for his human physiology and hygiene class at Cornell University and later by his son, Joel Myers Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various ephemera, programs, pamphlets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multi-year diary maintained by Arthur Cornelius Lansing, Class of 1921

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ephemera, postcards, letters, photographs relating to Cornell and Ithaca. Includes photo of Phi Sigma Kappa fraternity when used as barracks for the S.A.T.C., Dwellers Reunion program, Official Guide to the Schoellkopf Sweepstakes Spring Day 1923, items relating to Clarence Isaac Robinson, Class of 1884,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cabinet cards of Cornell students and a tintype of Taughannock Falls with people holding an umbrella.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cabinet cards, stereo views and other photographs, several relating to the Vermont Academy, Cornell students and dorm room images, Ithaca parade, Ithaca and Lucifer Falls.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Broadside from the Inter-Collegiate Contest of Greek, Latin, Mathematics & Essays competition. 3rd Annual Ovation of the Citizens of Ithaca, to Cornell's Victors! Held at Library Hall, William L. Bostwick with S. d. Halliday and interim President Russel, January 20, 1877

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on Geodesy, prepared for the Senior Class in Civil Engineering by Charles Lee Crandall Class of 1872, personal expenditure journal maintained by Arvin J. Dillenbeck, Class of 1911, laboratory notebook maintained by Harold Carl Harding, Class of 1910, for Course XII in mechanical engineering in the Sibley College, Department of Experimental Engineering

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1921 photo of the faculty and students of the Cornell University Chemistry Department, group portrait of the Class of 1895

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large collection of letters—many professing love and admiration—sent by 17 different service men during World War II to Marie Ann Tipula, Class of 1947, while she was a student at Cornell University and later when she returned home to Hoboken, NJ to work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circa 1890 photograph of the Peck Shoe Store in Cortland, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stereo view, cabinet cards, and other photographs of Cornell and the Ithaca area including bridges, Taughannock Hotel and Falls, Cornell weather station, images relating to Ithaca trolley cars and George W. Brownell. Ephemera, letters relating to Cornell students,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Broadside from the Class of 1919 Freshman Banquet. Held at the Armory March 1916

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cabinet cards of students and faculty mostly from the Class of 1873

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items relating to Clark Dillenbeck

did.unittitle: Names index, see PDF guide above for location.
